title: The Comeback Kid

jokes.text: Ann, you beautiful tropical fish.
jokes.setup: Leslie needs to compliment Ann
jokes.punchline: Compares her to a tropical fish in earnest admiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope moment - absurd metaphor delivered with complete sincerity. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: You've resuscitated a human heart in your bare hands. No, I haven't. You haven't? No. You will. You're that good of a nurse.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to praise Ann's medical skills
jokes.punchline: When corrected that Ann hasn't done this, Leslie insists she will because she's so good
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Leslie's inability to back down from a compliment even when proven wrong.

jokes.text: Again? You just hired me eight seconds ago. Wow, you're doing a really bad job.
jokes.setup: Leslie criticizes Ann for not wanting to discuss campaign manager duties 'again'
jokes.punchline: Ann points out the impossibility, Leslie immediately declares her terrible at the job
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Leslie's impossible standards and immediate disappointment.

jokes.text: I tried to make ramen in the coffee pot and I broke everything.
jokes.setup: Leslie is trying to impress former campaign advisors
jokes.punchline: Andy interrupts with completely inappropriate domestic disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - childlike incompetence at the worst possible time.

jokes.text: Are most murders committed by highly-trained, professional assassins? No. They're committed by friends and co-workers.
jokes.setup: Leslie needs to justify not having professional campaign managers
jokes.punchline: Compares campaign management to murder, then realizes the analogy is terrible
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark turn followed by immediate self-awareness that it didn't work.

jokes.text: That analogy was way better in my head.
jokes.setup: Leslie's murder analogy falls flat
jokes.punchline: She immediately acknowledges it didn't work
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment showing Leslie's self-awareness. Efficient and relatable.

jokes.text: His name is Champion, because he's the Dog World Champion.
jokes.setup: Andy explains the three-legged dog's name
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that a dog named Champion must be the champion
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy logic - nonsensical but delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: He can do more with three legs than most dogs can do with four. Except for digging. He's really bad at digging.
jokes.setup: Andy builds up Champion's abilities despite missing leg
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts it with specific limitation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with specific, unexpected detail about digging.

jokes.text: So you just say the word and Champion goes back to the pound where he can be put down and killed forever.
jokes.setup: Andy offering April control over the dog decision
jokes.punchline: Describes euthanization in the most dramatic terms possible
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered in Andy's innocent way, making it uncomfortable manipulation.

jokes.text: Cruel, but fair.
jokes.setup: April points out she and Andy have jobs unlike April
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact acceptance of the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan - acknowledging truth even when it's about her.

jokes.text: Well, well, well. Look who's ahead of the curve. I was joking. You should soak that in bleach and burn it.
jokes.setup: Tom appears to vindicate his Hawaiian shirt choice
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately clarifies she was mocking him
jokes.explanation: Good setup and immediate deflation, with Leslie's characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: Shut up, Ann. I told you never to talk to me. That was Champion. Oh, my God. I'm sorry. Bad dog.
jokes.setup: April rudely responds to Ann
jokes.punchline: Claims it was the dog talking, then scolds the dog
jokes.explanation: Creative way for April to be rude while maintaining plausible deniability, followed by commitment to the bit.

jokes.text: I don't want to paint with a broad brush here, but every single contractor in the world is a miserable, incompetent thief.
jokes.setup: Ron says he doesn't want to generalize
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes the most sweeping generalization possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - the irony of claiming not to generalize while doing exactly that. Excellent structure and character specificity.

jokes.text: No. I find calzone fatty and unnecessary.
jokes.setup: Ben offers Chris a calzone
jokes.punchline: Chris's blunt, health-focused rejection
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris Traeger health obsession delivered with his characteristic directness.

jokes.text: Ben is massively depressed. And he needs my help.
jokes.setup: Ben seems happy and engaged with his hobbies
jokes.punchline: Chris's talking head reveals Ben is actually severely depressed
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience realizes Ben's cheerfulness is actually concerning.

jokes.text: See? There's more things to look at on the Internet besides naked guys, Ann.
jokes.setup: Ann successfully googled what campaign managers wear
jokes.punchline: Leslie's inappropriate assumption about Ann's internet habits
jokes.explanation: Typical Leslie inappropriate overshare disguised as helpful advice.

jokes.text: Do your carpets match your drapes?
jokes.setup: Tom talking to carpet salesman about his business
jokes.punchline: Uses sexual innuendo phrase completely literally
jokes.explanation: Tom's oblivious use of innuendo in business context, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Red carpet insole. Everywhere I go, I'm walking on red carpet.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his extensive red carpet collection
jokes.punchline: Even his shoe insoles are red carpet for maximum luxury
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tom's materialistic obsessions to absurd logical extreme.

jokes.text: No, that's Champion's spot. He called it.
jokes.setup: Tom asks if he can sit on Andy's lap instead of April's
jokes.punchline: Andy treats the three-legged dog like it has equal rights to car seating
jokes.explanation: Andy's innocent anthropomorphizing of the dog, treating it as equal family member.

jokes.text: Yeah. Champion is a way better kisser than you are, babe. He's even a better kisser than me.
jokes.setup: Andy showing affection for the dog
jokes.punchline: Ranks all three of them by kissing ability, putting himself last
jokes.explanation: Andy's self-deprecating way of praising the dog, characteristic innocent weirdness.

jokes.text: Oh, look. The police even love it. They're sirening back to us.
jokes.setup: Police sirens start blaring behind their illegal truck ride
jokes.punchline: Andy interprets police pursuit as friendly communication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy obliviousness - completely misreading obvious danger as friendliness.

jokes.text: The jitterbug, stagecoaches, Herman Munster.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to convince Pete the past is great
jokes.punchline: Random list mixing dance, transportation, and TV monster
jokes.explanation: Leslie's characteristic random list-making, though not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Well, we have a philosophical difference on what constitutes a law.
jokes.setup: Officer lists multiple laws they've broken
jokes.punchline: Ron treats legal violations as mere philosophical disagreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson libertarian worldview - dismissing government authority with academic language.

jokes.text: My mother's butt.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks April where they are for location reference
jokes.punchline: April gives nonsensical, inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Pure April response - unhelpful and mildly offensive for no reason.

jokes.text: He must dunk, Ann. Do whatever it takes. Yeah, anything short of sexual favors.
jokes.setup: Leslie desperately needs Pete to dunk at the rally
jokes.punchline: Explicitly mentions sexual favors while claiming not to want them
jokes.explanation: Leslie's inappropriate boundary-setting that reveals she actually considered it.

jokes.text: Although, I am a little offended that you wouldn't do that.
jokes.setup: Ann says she wasn't going to offer sexual favors
jokes.punchline: Leslie is hurt that Ann won't prostitute herself for the campaign
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Leslie's warped priorities and friendship expectations.

jokes.text: What about pizza? Pizza? Never heard of it.
jokes.setup: Chris points out pizza exists as Italian fast food
jokes.punchline: Ben pretends pizza doesn't exist to support his calzone business
jokes.explanation: Ben's desperate denial of obvious competition, showing his delusion.

jokes.text: Pizza is your grandfather's calzone.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to position calzones as superior to pizza
jokes.punchline: Reverses typical 'your father's Oldsmobile' format with absurd food comparison
jokes.explanation: Creative inversion of advertising cliché applied to food rivalry.

jokes.text: The Low-Cal Calzone Zone.
jokes.setup: Ben describing his business concept
jokes.punchline: Terrible alliterative name that's hard to say
jokes.explanation: Memorably bad business name showing Ben's poor judgment in his depressed state.

jokes.text: That idea is literally the greatest idea I have ever heard in my life.
jokes.setup: Ben presents obviously terrible business idea
jokes.punchline: Chris's characteristic over-enthusiasm for something objectively bad
jokes.explanation: Chris's relentless positivity applied to obviously terrible idea.

jokes.text: That idea is terrible.
jokes.setup: Chris just called it the greatest idea ever
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself in talking head
jokes.explanation: Perfect mockumentary format joke - Chris's private honesty vs. public encouragement.

jokes.text: And that girl, she tried to get that gimp dog to bite me.
jokes.setup: Officer explaining why he's upset with the group
jokes.punchline: April sicced the three-legged dog on a police officer
jokes.explanation: Very April thing to do - using disabled dog as weapon against authority.

jokes.text: You know that I'm going to pay all the fines. I'm in the middle of a campaign rally. And this is my team. I need them. Please.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to get her team out of jail
jokes.punchline: Calling this dysfunctional group her essential campaign 'team'
jokes.explanation: Ironic given how incompetent the team has proven to be.

jokes.text: Sometimes, life dunks you.
jokes.setup: Pete reflecting on being defined by one basketball moment
jokes.punchline: Reverses basketball metaphor to describe life's hardships
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of basketball terminology for existential reflection.

jokes.text: With my claymaish? With your life. You are wildly, insanely depressed.
jokes.setup: Chris says Ben needs help
jokes.punchline: Ben thinks he means with his made-up art form, Chris means with depression
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Ben's complete lack of self-awareness about his mental state.

jokes.text: Do you think a depressed person could make this?
jokes.setup: Ben defending his mental health
jokes.punchline: Points to terrible claymation as evidence he's fine
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - the terrible claymation actually proves he IS depressed.

jokes.text: He shall do it in the car.
jokes.setup: Champion needs to use the bathroom
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact acceptance that dog will defecate in car
jokes.explanation: April's characteristic nihilistic acceptance of disgusting outcomes.

jokes.text: In fact, I... I e-mailed Leslie two days ago and I compared it to Avatar, Chris.
jokes.setup: Ben realizes his claymation video is terrible
jokes.punchline: Reveals he compared 3 seconds of amateur claymation to blockbuster movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - reveals the depth of Ben's delusion about his artistic abilities.

jokes.text: It's not a basketball court anymore. It's an ice skating rink.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks for status report on basketball-themed rally venue
jokes.punchline: April reveals fundamental impossibility of basketball event
jokes.explanation: Major obstacle reveal that escalates the disaster, though not particularly clever.

jokes.text: And, either way, it was his fault, because he was stupid and I hated him.
jokes.setup: April explaining why she didn't listen to venue instructions
jokes.punchline: Blames venue worker for her own negligence with flawless April logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect April reasoning - refuses responsibility and makes it about personal animosity.

jokes.text: We had to Jetsons most of the poster, too.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining what happened to Leslie's campaign poster
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Jetsons' as verb meaning to jettison, with cartoon family reference
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay turning cartoon family name into technical term.

jokes.text: Because windows are the eyes to the house.
jokes.setup: Jerry looking at partial campaign poster
jokes.punchline: Offers completely irrelevant architectural philosophy
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry non-sequitur - profound-sounding but completely unhelpful.

jokes.text: Anyway, the important news is that he's here. Good. Is he going to dunk for me? I'm not sure. I couldn't hear through all the crying.
jokes.setup: Ann reports success getting Pete to the rally
jokes.punchline: Reveals both she and Pete were crying, unclear who was crying about what
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the emotional disaster surrounding the simple request.

jokes.text: Oh, damn it, Jerry. You just had to do your job, didn't you?
jokes.setup: Jerry successfully got 100 people to attend the rally
jokes.punchline: Leslie is angry at Jerry for being competent when she wanted to cancel
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - blaming someone for doing their job well when you need failure.

jokes.text: I couldn't afford enough premium carpet to get us to the stage. I mean, it was a short walk, but it was pretty luxurious, right?
jokes.setup: Tom promised a red carpet entrance to the stage
jokes.punchline: Could only afford about 3 feet of red carpet, tries to spin it positively
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment - materialistic aspirations meeting budget reality with spin.

jokes.text: Babe! Are you okay? He is peeing. He is now peeing. Now, I'm putting him down.
jokes.setup: Andy falls off stage, Ron catches the dog
jokes.punchline: Dog pees on Ron while he's holding it, Ron's matter-of-fact narration
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation with Ron's deadpan narration of disgusting situation.

jokes.text: Ron? Ron? There's no stairs. How do I get onto the stage?
jokes.setup: Tom needs to get on the tiny stage Ron built
jokes.punchline: Stage is too high and has no access method
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy problem escalating the rally disaster.

jokes.text: Ugh! It tastes like a belt.
jokes.setup: Chris gives Ben herbal remedy from his 'herb belt'
jokes.punchline: Ben's literal interpretation of the taste
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation of Chris's hippie health solutions.

jokes.text: The Letters to Cleo T-shirt, the unshaven face, the Doc Martens... And your hair does not have that normal uptight, rigid, inflexible Ben Wyatt sense of fun.
jokes.setup: Chris lists signs of Ben's depression
jokes.punchline: Describes Ben's normal rigid personality as 'fun'
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Chris sees Ben's uptightness as a positive trait.

jokes.text: Together, we can defeat obese children.
jokes.setup: Leslie's campaign speech cards get mixed up
jokes.punchline: Accidentally suggests fighting children instead of childhood obesity
jokes.explanation: Good accidental dark humor from speech mix-up, very quotable.

jokes.text: Well, I can assure you people in the bleachers that if you follow my campaign, it will be interesting.
jokes.setup: Leslie's rally has been a complete disaster
jokes.punchline: Promises 'interesting' campaign after demonstrating chaos
jokes.explanation: Good meta-acknowledgment of the disaster while maintaining political optimism.

jokes.text: Pistol Pete, everybody. Still got it.
jokes.setup: Pete attempts actual dunk after speech
jokes.punchline: Leslie tries to spin his obvious failure positively
jokes.explanation: Good political spin on obvious athletic failure, classic Leslie optimism.

jokes.text: This team has a lot of heart and zero know-how.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her campaign team to Ben
jokes.punchline: Perfectly summarizes their incompetent enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of the team's well-meaning incompetence in efficient phrase.

jokes.text: Oh! Don't make out. It's making Champion sad.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben embrace after he agrees to be campaign manager
jokes.punchline: Andy anthropomorphizes dog's reaction to romantic affection
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy projection of complex emotions onto the dog.

jokes.text: You're not married. She's not married.
jokes.setup: Pete asks Ann to dinner, she claims to be married
jokes.punchline: Ann insists on the obvious lie while Leslie contradicts her
jokes.explanation: Basic awkward dating situation escalation, not particularly clever.

jokes.text: This is uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Awkward exchange about Ann's relationship status
jokes.punchline: Pete states the obvious in understated way
jokes.explanation: Basic acknowledgment of awkwardness, efficient but not creative.

tmdb_overview: Leslie enlists everyone's help in staging an attention-getting event. Ben takes up a new hobby, while April and Andy adopt a pet.
insight: Ben's campaign manager arc escalates absurdist humor faster than the plot can follow.
